data.narrative:
OroraTech is a Series B space and aerospace company that has raised €38M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector private companies: Aalyria ($1.3B), Hubble Network ($400M), and Sift ($274M). A broader reference universe includes five public space and aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) trading at a median 53.2x price-to-sales, though this method cannot be applied directly without OroraTech's disclosed revenue.

The peer-median approach is the primary applicable method. The three private comparables exhibit a wide range from $274M to $1.3B, with a median valuation of $400M. This dispersion reflects material differences in business model maturity and market traction within the space sector at Series B stage. Public-comp anchoring is not executable given the absence of target revenue disclosure, typical for early-stage private companies.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $340M to $460M, centered on $400M. This narrow band reflects reliance on a single methodological leg and limited peer sample size. Key uncertainties include the degree to which OroraTech's revenue traction and unit economics align with the selected private peers, and the broader question of whether public sector comparables (median 53x P/S) would yield material validation if revenue were available. Substantive downside or upside would require clarity on growth trajectory and capital efficiency relative to Aalyria, Hubble Network, and Sift.
